stem: A team of three programmers is starting work on an online quiz application.
model_answer: Decomposition splits a large problem into smaller, more manageable sub-problems. For the quiz app, the team can decompose the system into login/account, question display, marking, and results storage. Each sub-problem can be assigned to a different programmer, tested independently and joined together at the end. Smaller sub-problems are easier to reason about and bugs are less likely to cross module boundaries.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Award one mark for the definition and one mark for a benefit applied to the team / project context.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Explain what decomposition means and why it is useful for the quiz-app team.

mark_points.text: decomposition splits a problem into smaller sub-problems / modules
benefit applied to a team (parallel work / each module easier to test / bugs isolated)
